mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply chain, which in turn boosts demand for S&P 500 equities, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals a tightening labor market, which in turn boosts wage growth and increases the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 700,000 signals an expansion in the supply chain, which in turn boosts demand for S&P 500 equities, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4% signals an expansion in consumer demand, which in turn boosts demand for S&P 500 equities, producing positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals a tightening labor market, which in turn boosts wage growth and increases the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle, which in turn compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals an expansion in the economy, which in turn boosts demand for S&P 500 equities, producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
